learning_outcomes.description: Recognise and classify types of meaning in language
Appreciate how different languages might encode meaning
Examine meaning relative to spatial, temporal and interpersonal contexts
Explore the connection between meaning and thought
Apply semantic analyses to real-world examples of language use

assessments.task_description:
This assessment is designed to help you prepare for your essay by critically reading relevant literature.

 For the literature summary, you will:

  • Choose your essay topic for the "Essay" assessment
  • Read the paper assigned for your topic
  • Choose one additional academic source relevant to your essay topic
  • Use the template provided to make a summary of both papers
  • Write three discussion questions that you could raise with your group in Assessment 2: In class discussion and reflection

You will use your literature summary as a starting point for Assessment 2: In class discussion and reflection.

More details and instructions for this assessment will be given in class at the beginning of semester.

AI and MT Statement

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
8/09/2026 8:00 am

This assessment is conducted IN CLASS at 8am in WEEK 7.

This assessment must be completed in-person.
assessments.task_description:
This assessment is designed to help you prepare for your essay by discussing relevant literature with classmates, and reflecting on this process.

You will use the literature summary and discussion questions you developed for Assessment 1 as a starting point for your group discussions.

For the discussion and reflection, you will:

  • Be allocated to a small group with other students who have chosen the same essay topic
  • Discuss the literature you have read with your small group
  • As a group, share the main points of your discussion with the whole class. Each member of the group will be responsible for reporting one aspect of the discussion to the class.
  • Individually, answer written reflection questions (provided in class), about how your understanding of the literature has developed, and about your experience of the group discussion.

This assessment will be marked individually, based on your participation in reporting the discussion to the class, and on your written reflection.

More details and instructions for this assessment will be given in class at the beginning of semester.

AI and MT Statement

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Written reflections to be submitted in class.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
For this assessment, you will write an essay on a Semantics topic from one of the areas covered in Weeks 1-6.

You will be given a list of essay topics to choose from in Week 1.

The word limit for your essay is 2000 words (+/- 10%).

Suggestions for researching, structuring and writing your essay will be discussed in class.

AI and MT Statement

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
